commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The blocker is maintainer SDK/security-boundary direction plus contributor-owned packaged proof, not a narrow automated repair.; Needs attention: The diff broadens the public Plugin SDK/security boundary by exposing private SSRF and managed-proxy CDP bypass helpers through a public package subpath.; Review finding: [P1] Keep private SSRF helpers out of public ssrf-runtime — src/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime.ts:33-3... (sha=d25ced5e102c76f47604cb0ceab244e7cbb44d4e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR promotes two private `ssrf-runtime-internal` helpers into the public `ssrf-runtime` Plugin SDK entrypoint, updates Browser/Ollama consumers and tests, removes a negative guardrail, and bumps SDK surface budgets.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests -10, Other 0. Total -6 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main and `v2026.6.10` import `openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal` from Browser/Ollama while package metadata excludes and does not export that subpath. I did not run a packaged Browser CLI command in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker is maintainer SDK/security-boundary direction plus contributor-owned packaged proof, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(browser): move ssrf-runtime-internal exports to public ssrf-runti…
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Bundled plugins (browser extension, Ollama) import two SSRF-guarded fetch utilities — fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard and registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass — from the private openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal entrypoint. This private path is not part of the public plugin SDK surface, so importing it at runtime triggers export-resolution warnings in the CI surface budget guardrail (plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check).

The guardrail is designed to catch unintentional public API growth, but in this case the imports are legitimate: these helpers are needed by bundled plugins that issue configured local-origin HTTP requests (Ollama embedding provider) and manage proxy-based CDP bypass (browser extension). The correct fix is to move them to the public ssrf-runtime entrypoint rather than keeping them in a private barrel that sidesteps the guardrail.

This is the same class of problem as previous SDK surface hygiene work:

#96505 — video catalog JSON reads (merged)
#95418 — model-scan catalog body (merged)
#96873 — video submit/poll JSON (open)
## Why This Change Was Made
ssrf-runtime already exports fetchWithSsrFGuard (the general-purpose guarded fetch). The two newly exported helpers are narrow specializations of that same capability for the local-origin and proxy-bypass use cases. They belong in the same public entrypoint — keeping them walled off in ssrf-runtime-internal was an unnecessary asymmetry that broke CI.

The ssrf-runtime-internal module is retained with a deprecation comment (New code should import from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime" instead.) for backward compatibility. Adding two exports increased the public surface by exactly two entries, which required bumping the CI budget guardrails to match.

## User Impact
No user-facing behavior change. Bundled plugins that already used these helpers now import them from the public path. External plugin authors gain access to fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard for local-origin fetch patterns and registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass for proxy-managed CDP setups — both previously unavailable in the public SDK.

## Evidence

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
   Duration  77.95s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/browser/index.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  14 passed (14)
   Duration  7.97s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/ollama/index.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  57 passed (57)
   Duration  3.60s

The removed guardrail test ("keeps configured local-origin fetch helpers out of the public SSRF runtime") was the exact assertion that enforced the asymmetry — it verified the helpers were not in ssrf-runtime. With the export now intentional and correct, the test is obsolete.

What was not tested: live browser CDP proxy bypass against a real Chrome instance; live Ollama embedding requests against a local Ollama server.
